mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real estate financing (RSAFS) signals strong household wealth accumulation and consumer spending, which drives S&P 500 forward returns through increased corporate revenue and risk appetite.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth exceeds 2% YoY but S&P 500 20d returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate indicates monetary easing, reducing borrowing costs and equity discount rates, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS declines but S&P 500 20d returns are negative in > 60% of cases, or forward rate curve inverts without equity underperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust supply-side capacity and demand, boosting corporate margins and stock valuations through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST both rise > 1.5% MoM but S&P 500 20d returns are negative, or pairwise correlation < 0.3 in 12mo rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) signals tightening labor markets, which pressures wages and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rise but S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or wage growth < 3% despite labor market strength.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 2%) creates stagflationary risks, which depress S&P 500 returns through heightened uncertainty and reduced monetary policy flexibility.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS in this regime but S&P 500 60d returns are positive in > 60% of cases, or real rates remain stable despite inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines but S&P 500 60d returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or leading indicators show divergent economic health.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases but S&P 500 20d returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or jobless claims decline despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, which reduces corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines but S&P 500 5d returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or input costs remain stable despite production declines.
